dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3625 IS: Qualified Immunity Abolition Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Revised Statutes to remove the defense of qualified immunity in the case of any action under section 1979, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Qualified Immunity Abolition Act of 2026.
Section 1979 of the Revised Statutes (42 U.S.C. 1983) is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting (a) before Every person; and
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: It shall not be a defense to any action brought against a Federal, State, or local law enforcement officer that is pending on, or filed after, the date of enactment of this subsection that, at the time of the deprivation—

paragraph.text: the defendant was acting in good faith;
the defendant believed, reasonably or otherwise, that his or her conduct was lawful;
the rights, privileges, or immunities secured by the Constitution and laws were not clearly established; or
the state of the law was such that the defendant could not reasonably have been expected to know whether his or her conduct was lawful.

section.header: Civil action against Federal law enforcement officers acting under Federal authority
section.text: Subsection (a) of section 1979 of the Revised Statutes (42 U.S.C. 1983), as so designated by section 4 of this Act, is amended by inserting and every Federal law enforcement officer who, under color of any statute, ordinance, regulation, custom, or usage of the United States, before subjects.
